data.title: WLFI co-founder: Reports of users' wallets being frozen are completely misleading.
data.description: Zach Witkoff, co-founder of WLFI, the Trump family's crypto project, responded on the X platform to reports of users' wallets being frozen and forced to undergo KYC, stating that the reports were completely misleading and that WLFI always supports community members who have suffered from phishing attacks.

data.title: Analysts predict four rate cuts next year after the new Federal Reserve chairman takes office.
data.description: Infrastructure Capital analyst Jay Hatfield said he didn't expect the Federal Reserve to cut rates in December unless the jobs data was very weak, but the data completely contradicted his team's expectations. "We still expect the Fed to hold rates steady in December. We are confident that inflation is gradually declining and that there will be four rate cuts next year after the new Fed chairman takes office. Therefore, the 10-year Treasury yield should remain around 4%, which is good for the stock market." (Jinshi)

data.title: Opinion: The Fed's Beige Book may once again be a decisive factor in interest rate cuts.
data.description: Institutional analysts' comments on the US September non-farm payrolls: Assessing the probability of a Fed rate cut in December is difficult. Market pricing is again close to a 50% probability, and the Beige Book may once again be the deciding factor, as no new CPI or employment reports will be available before the December meeting. For long-term interest rates, if the Fed directs market attention to a January rate cut at its meeting, then a December rate cut will be irrelevant. Therefore, in my view, all the concerns surrounding whether there will be a rate cut in December are only meaningful for short-term interest rate traders. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysts: The Fed has room to wait for more data; market reactions to the non-farm payroll report have been mixed.
data.description: Seema Shah, an analyst at Principal Asset Management, commented on the US September non-farm payrolls report: Market reactions to the report were mixed. Stocks welcomed the stronger-than-expected job growth, indicating a robust economy; while the bond market focused more on rising unemployment and slowing wage growth, which could keep the possibility of a Fed rate cut in December alive. Today's jobs data is unlikely to incentivize the Fed to cut rates in December. Even so, holding rates steady in December wouldn't be a major mistake. While weak consumer confidence highlights concerns about job security, the labor market, though soft, is by no means in recession, giving the Fed breathing room to act decisively only after gathering sufficiently compelling data. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 91,000 USDT with a 0.51% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Nov 20, 2025, 14:08 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 91,000 USDT and is now trading at 90,846.453125 USDT, with a narrowed 0.51% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: SoftBank to Invest Up to $3 Billion in OpenAI Data Center
data.description: According to Foresight News, The Information reports that SoftBank plans to invest up to $3 billion in an OpenAI data center facility. This significant investment underscores SoftBank's commitment to advancing artificial intelligence infrastructure.

data.title: Nillion: Will repurchase NIL tokens sold without authorization by a certain market maker and pursue legal action against them.
data.description: Nillion officially disclosed that yesterday the project experienced an unauthorized sale of NIL tokens by a market maker without legal authorization from the Nillion Association. The market maker refused to respond to any communication from the team during the flash crash and for several hours afterward. The Nillion Association has begun using government funds to buy back the sold tokens and has pledged to use all funds recovered from the unauthorized sale to purchase more NIL tokens. The association stated that it is pursuing the matter fully within the legal framework and is working overnight with exchange partners to ensure that all accounts and wallets related to this incident have been frozen.

data.title: BTC briefly dipped below 91,000 USDT, a 0.32% decrease in the last 24 hours.
data.description: OKX market data shows that BTC briefly dipped below 91,000 USDT, currently trading at 90,975.4 USDT, a 24-hour decrease of 0.32%. (This news item was generated with AI assistance.)

data.title: FG NEXUS currently holds 40,005 ETH, a decrease of approximately 10,000 ETH compared to the end of the third quarter.
data.description: Ethereum treasury company FG NEXUS released its Q3 financial report, disclosing that it held 50,778 ETH at the end of Q3, but as of November 19, it held 40,005 ETH, a reduction of approximately 10,000 ETH. The company also disclosed holding approximately $37 million worth of cash and USDC. (Globenewswire)

data.title: The address "Aave, which has been holding over $80 million worth of WBTC in a cyclical long position," reduced its holdings for the first time in six months, transferring 150 WBTC to Binance.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, an address that held over $80 million worth of WBTC long positions through Aave has begun unleveraging, marking its first reduction in holdings in six months. Between May and July 2025, it withdrew a total of 700.19 WBTC (approximately $81.63 million) from Binance, with an average long price of $116,593. Three hours ago, it deposited 150 WBTC into Binance for the first time; selling these would result in a loss of $3.734 million. Currently, it still has 550.2 WBTC pledged on Aave to borrow $28.09 million worth of stablecoins, with a health score of 1.4. A drop to $65,436 would trigger liquidation.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Executive Suggests Potential December Rate Cut by Federal Reserve
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Haith, the head of fixed income at Goldman Sachs Asset Management, indicated that the possibility of a Federal Reserve rate cut in December remains due to the ongoing weakness in the labor market, as reflected by the unemployment rate.

data.title: DDC Enterprise has reached a purchase agreement to acquire 300 more Bitcoins.
data.description: DDC Enterprise, a US-listed company, announced it has reached a purchase agreement to acquire 300 bitcoins. This is reportedly the company's largest single bitcoin acquisition to date. Upon completion of the transaction, DDC Enterprise's total bitcoin holdings will increase to 1,383 bitcoins, and the company projects a 99% return on its bitcoin holdings by the second half of 2025. (Businesswire)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Hamak: Further rate cuts could pose risks to financial stability.
data.description: Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Hamak warned on Thursday that further interest rate cuts at this time would pose broad risks to the economy. Given that inflation continues to rise above the Fed's 2% target, "lowering interest rates to support the labor market could prolong this period of high inflation and could also encourage risky behavior in financial markets," Hamak noted. She pointed out that current financial conditions are "quite loose" given rising stock prices and "easy" credit conditions, explaining that further reductions in credit costs under these circumstances "could support high-risk lending." (Jinshi)

data.title: Goldman Sachs: The possibility of a December rate cut still exists.
data.description: Goldman Sachs Asset Management's head of fixed income, Heinrich Hayes, stated, "Given the continued weakness in the labor market reflected in the unemployment rate, the possibility of a Fed rate cut in December remains." (Jinshi)

data.title: Brother Machi increased his long position in Ethereum to 3930 ETH, but is still in a loss-making state.
data.description: Hyperbot data shows that Brother Machi has just added to his 25x Ethereum long position, and his position has now increased to 3,930 ETH, worth $11.78 million, but he is still at a loss, with a liquidation price of $2,942.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Harker Warns Against Potential Risks of Interest Rate Cuts
data.description: According to Odaily, Federal Reserve official Harker has expressed concerns that cutting interest rates could prolong high inflation and encourage risky behavior in financial markets. He noted that banks currently have sufficient capital. Harker warned that reducing rates at this time might distort market pricing levels.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Hammack Highlights Concerns Over Stablecoins and Private Credit
data.description: According to Odaily, Federal Reserve's Hammack has expressed concerns regarding stablecoins and private credit, emphasizing their significance in the current financial landscape. He also pointed out that hedge funds and life insurance companies are operating with high leverage levels. The financial environment is described as 'quite loose,' and Hammack cautioned that lowering interest rates at this time could distort market pricing. Additionally, he warned that rate cuts might prolong the period of high inflation and could potentially increase financial stability risks if done for risk management purposes.

data.title: U.S. September Employment Figures Surpass Expectations
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the United States reported a significant increase in seasonally adjusted non-farm employment figures for September, reaching 119,000 jobs. This number exceeded the anticipated 50,000 jobs and marked a revision from the previous figure of 22,000 jobs, which was adjusted to a decrease of 4,000 jobs.

data.title: U.S. Labor Market Shows Signs of Weakness Amid AI Impact and Trade Policies
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the U.S. labor market exhibited signs of weakness as nonfarm employment growth accelerated in September, yet the unemployment rate rose to 4.4%. The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that nonfarm jobs increased by 119,000 in September, following a downward revision of August's figures to a decrease of 4,000 jobs. Economists surveyed by Reuters had initially predicted a rise of 50,000 jobs in September after an initial increase of 22,000 in August.

Economists estimate that the current economy requires only 30,000 to 50,000 new jobs monthly to accommodate the growth of the working-age population, which is significantly lower than the approximately 150,000 needed in 2024.

The unemployment rate increased from 4.3% in August to 4.4%. The growing prevalence of artificial intelligence is impacting labor demand, particularly affecting entry-level positions and leaving recent college graduates struggling to find employment. Economists highlight that AI is driving economic growth without corresponding job creation.

Additionally, some experts argue that U.S. President Donald Trump's trade policies have created an uncertain economic environment, undermining businesses' ability to hire, especially small enterprises.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Hamak: Rate cuts could prolong high inflation.
data.description: Federal Reserve's Hamack: Rate cuts could prolong high inflation and encourage risk-taking in financial markets; banks are currently well-capitalized. Cutting rates now could distort market pricing levels. (Jinshi)
